landmarks.name: The Citadel
landmarks.visualDescription: A massive stone facade with grand columns and arched windows, now pockmarked by artillery and decay. Water stains and mold streak the pale sandstone. Three tiers of ornate balconies collapse inward; rubble chokes the grand entrance. A single wing remains partially intact, its dome half-caved. Wooden scaffolding and salvage nets cover exposed sections. Floodwater laps at the base, reflecting broken glass and twisted metal railings.
landmarks.originalDescription: Royal Palace on Dam Square, a 17th-century Baroque civic building serving as Amsterdam's seat of government and cultural icon.
landmarks.meshyPrompt: Devastated 17th-century Baroque palace facade with pale sandstone, broken columns, collapsed balconies, water stains, mold, rubble heap entrance, partial dome damage, exposed metal framework, wooden salvage scaffolding, half-submerged stone base. Low-poly game asset.

landmarks.name: Windmill Tower
landmarks.visualDescription: A cylindrical brick tower, originally octagonal, now partially collapsed on one side. The wooden sails are splintered and missing. Moss and algae coat the red-brown brick from years of spray and flooding. The internal grinding machinery is rusted and exposed through large cracks. Water level marks show repeated flood cycles. A metal band halfway up is the only intact original fixture. Corroded bolts and chains dangle from the damaged roof.
landmarks.originalDescription: De Adriaan Windmill, an 18th-century grain mill iconic to Dutch heritage, historically used for pumping and milling in Amsterdam's water management system.
landmarks.meshyPrompt: Ruined 18th-century octagonal brick windmill tower, red-brown deteriorated brick, missing sails, collapsed wall section, exposed corroded metal gears, algae coating, water flood marks, rusty chains, half-submerged foundation, wooden structural beams visible. Low-poly game asset.

landmarks.name: Market Hall Ruins
landmarks.visualDescription: A sprawling iron-frame market hall with a corrugated metal roof, now punctured with dozens of holes. Rust blooms in orange and red streaks across the iron beams. Market stalls are toppled and crushed beneath fallen roof panels. Vines and mold cover every surface. The ground is a maze of twisted metal shelving, rotted wood crates, and abandoned goods encrusted with algae. Water pools in deep ruts between stall foundations. A few intact support pillars still bear faded painted signs. Skeletal remains of canvas awnings flutter from broken frames.
landmarks.originalDescription: Albert Cuyp Market, a 19th-century covered market arcade built for street traders, a bustling commercial heart of Amsterdam's daily commerce.
landmarks.meshyPrompt: Devastated 19th-century market hall, corrugated metal roof with holes, rust-streaked iron beams, collapsed stalls, twisted metal shelving, rotted wooden crates, algae-covered surfaces, water pooling, faded signs, skeletal canvas awnings, debris scattered, post-industrial decay. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Canal Trade Post
keyLocations.visualDescription: A narrow brick warehouse with a gabled roof and large loading doors on the canal-facing wall. The facade is badly water-damaged, with faded merchant guild insignias barely visible beneath moss. The loading hoist above the door is rusted but still mounted. Crates are stacked haphazardly on the stone dock. The waterline is visibly higher than the original floor level. Rope and chains hang from pulleys. The interior glows with warm lantern light despite boarded windows.
keyLocations.originalDescription: Historic warehouse converted into a floating trade dock, typical of Amsterdam's 17th-century merchant infrastructure along the canals.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: Weathered 17th-century brick warehouse with gabled roof, faded guild markings, moss-covered facade, large loading doors, rusted hoist mechanism, water-stained brick, stacked cargo crates, rope and pulley system, boarded windows with warm interior glow. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Salvage Guild
keyLocations.visualDescription: A brutalist concrete structure with reinforced walls and small high windows. Exterior walls are cracked and patched with welded metal sheets. Industrial shelving is visible through open delivery doors. Rows of metal containers and crates are organized in neat rows inside. The roof bears faded company logos and warning signs. Chain-link fencing with razor wire surrounds the perimeter. Loading ramps are reinforced with wooden planks. The ground is stained with unknown substances.
keyLocations.originalDescription: Former industrial processing facility and storage complex from the early 20th century, now repurposed as a secure depot.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: Brutalist 20th-century industrial warehouse, concrete walls with cracks, reinforced metal patches, corrugated metal fencing, razor wire, metal containers and shelving, faded logos, loading ramps, chemical stains, heavy industrial aesthetic. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Medic's Haven
keyLocations.visualDescription: A stone-fronted building with tall arched windows and a distinctive ornate entrance portico. The facade bears carved emblems of medicine (caduceus symbols). Many windows are broken or patched with cloth. A handmade red-cross flag hangs above the entrance. Makeshift beds are visible through broken windows. Bottles and medical containers line external shelves. The courtyard entrance shows signs of hasty fortification. Dried herbs and plants are hung from the eaves for drying.
keyLocations.originalDescription: Former 19th-century apothecary and civic medical clinic, historically the primary health facility for Amsterdam's working districts.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 19th-century stone-fronted clinic with arched windows, carved medical emblems, patched and broken windows, red cross flag, dried medicinal herbs hanging from eaves, broken glass, cloth patches, handmade fortifications, weathered stone facade. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: The Compass
keyLocations.visualDescription: A narrow, multi-story townhouse with a red-brick facade and a distinctive round bay window on each level. The roof is steeply pitched with decorative gables. Large maps and charts are visible through intact ground-floor windows. Brass navigational instruments hang in the window displays. The front door is reinforced with crossbars. Weathered ropes and ship rigging is coiled on the front stoop. A faded sign reading 'Guild of the Compass' is barely readable above the entrance.
keyLocations.originalDescription: A 17th-century cartography and navigation guild hall, historically a meeting place for sailors, merchants, and explorers planning expeditions.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 17th-century red-brick navigator's guild hall, narrow facade, round bay windows, steep pitched roof, decorative gables, brass instruments displayed, ship ropes and rigging, reinforced door, faded guild signage, weathered wood trim. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Smith's Workshop
keyLocations.visualDescription: A large brick building with a high ceiling and continuous strip windows along one wall. Massive furnaces and anvils are visible through an open garage door. Stacks of metal scrap and half-finished tools litter the workshop floor. Chains and pulleys hang from a reinforced ceiling beam. The brick exterior is heavily scored with soot and heat marks. A large chimney extends upward, venting thin smoke. Piles of ore and coke are stacked outside. Welding torches and hand-forged tools hang from pegboards.
keyLocations.originalDescription: Industrial metalworking foundry from the 19th century, historically producing hardware, tools, and structural components for Amsterdam's infrastructure.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 19th-century brick metalworking foundry, high industrial ceiling, furnaces and anvils visible, soot-stained brick, massive chimney, metal scrap piles, chains and pulleys overhead, reinforced beam structure, welding equipment, coal storage, heavy industrial aesthetic. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: The Anchor
keyLocations.visualDescription: A three-story building with timber framing and plaster infill, typical of old Dutch maritime taverns. The ground floor has large windows and a heavy wooden door reinforced with iron bands. Signs of former prosperity are visible in the carved beam ends and decorative shutters (now hanging askew). The interior glows amber with firelight. Fishing nets and maritime memorabilia hang from the walls inside. The roof is patched with multiple repairs. Broken ship lanterns are repurposed as lighting. A homemade sign with an anchor carved in wood swings above the entrance.
keyLocations.originalDescription: Historic 18th-century harbour tavern and sailors' lodging house, a traditional gathering place for maritime workers and merchants.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 18th-century timber-frame Dutch tavern, plaster infill, reinforced wooden door with iron bands, broken shutters, carved beam details, fishing nets and ship memorabilia visible through windows, repurposed ship lanterns, patched roof, anchor sign overhead. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Guard Post
keyLocations.visualDescription: A rectangular stone military building with symmetrical fenestration and a central watchtower. Arrow slits have been widened into proper windows. The facade bears faded military insignias and patrol route markers. Sandbags and barricades line the entrance and roofline. Watchtowers have been reinforced with metal bracing. Interior walls visible through broken sections show bunk arrangements and storage for weapons. A raised flagpole (now bare) marks the roof. Defensive pikes and spears are arranged in racks near the entrance.
keyLocations.originalDescription: Former 19th-century civic barracks and garrison building, historically housing Amsterdam's municipal armed forces and security personnel.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 19th-century stone barracks with central watchtower, arrow slits converted to windows, faded military insignia, sandbag barricades, reinforced watchtower, symmetrical facade, metal bracing, weapons racks, bare flagpole, defensive fortifications. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Flower Exchange
keyLocations.visualDescription: A narrow covered arcade with a glass roof (now mostly shattered). Market stalls with remnants of colorful signage line both sides. Dried flowers and plant specimens are preserved in jars on hastily organized shelves. Packets of seeds (both preserved and growing from cracks) still bear faded Dutch and Latin labels. The ground is carpeted with dried petals and seed husks. A few hardy plants have reclaimed sections of the stalls, vines climbing the internal supports. Lanterns provide gentle illumination. The smell of dried flowers and soil is strong.
keyLocations.originalDescription: The historic Amsterdam Flower Market (Bloemenmarkt), a 19th-century covered market specializing in bulbs, seeds, and cut flowers.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 19th-century glass-roofed flower market arcade, shattered glass panels, market stalls with faded signage, seed packets and dried flower specimens in jars, reclaiming vines, dried petals carpeting, lantern lighting, botanical aesthetic, weathered timber and glass framework. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Water Mill
keyLocations.visualDescription: A timber-frame structure built directly over a canal lock system. A large wooden waterwheel (now stationary) dominates the canal-facing side. The wheel is partially submerged in the flooded water. Internal gears and millstones are visible through open sections. The building sags from water damage but remains structurally sound. Wooden scaffolding props up the waterwheel. Channels and sluices direct water flow around the structure. Faded guild marks and maintenance dates are carved into the door frame. Tools and grain sacks are stored in the upper floors.
keyLocations.originalDescription: A 17th-century functional water mill converted to serve Amsterdam's grain milling and power generation needs via canal water.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 17th-century water mill timber structure over canal lock, large wooden waterwheel partially submerged, visible internal gears and millstones, timber-frame construction, wooden scaffolding support, sluice gates, faded guild markings, grain storage, water-damaged but stable. Low-poly game asset.

keyLocations.name: Bridge Archive
keyLocations.visualDescription: A stately neoclassical structure with tall columns and pediment details. Large windows (some boarded) once provided reading light. Interior shelves are densely packed with books, documents, and maps in various states of preservation. Wooden card catalogs and reading desks are intact but weathered. Water damage has affected lower shelves; higher sections are protected. Lanterns and salvaged light sources illuminate the interior. A grand stone staircase leads to upper gallery floors. Maps and nautical charts are pinned to remaining walls. The roof shows signs of careful patching.
keyLocations.originalDescription: The historic Amsterdam Municipal Library and Archives, a 19th-century neoclassical building housing historical records and civic knowledge.
keyLocations.meshyPrompt: 19th-century neoclassical library with tall columns, pediment detail, dense bookshelves and archives, wooden card catalogs, reading desks, water-stained lower shelves, protected upper galleries, grand stone staircase, maps and charts, lantern lighting, careful repairs. Low-poly game asset.

hazards.description: Canals have overflowed catastrophically; standing water levels remain dangerously high even in low-lying streets and districts. Tidal surges and breaches in the dike system continue to flood areas unpredictably. Underwater debris and sudden drop-offs create drowning hazards.

Structural failures are widespread; weakened buildings show signs of partial collapse. The collapse of a major dike section has compromised load-bearing walls in adjacent districts. Rubble piles block some routes; unstable rooflines threaten sudden failure.
hazards.locationHint: centre and north sectors, particularly near the Citadel and Market Hall

hazards.description: Industrial contamination has poisoned the canal water system; chemical stains and unidentified substances coat the water surface. Noxious fumes rise from certain canal sections. Drinking or prolonged exposure to the water causes illness. Protective gear is essential in affected zones.
hazards.locationHint: east canal industrial sector and flooded warehouse district

spatialSpec.terrainDescription: Flat delta terrain with extensive water management; predominantly water-logged after collapse of dike infrastructure. Saturated ground with standing water in low-lying districts.
